description: Gate-driven corpus for container-security: each fixture is a Dockerfile run through the same PolicyCheck the CI gate uses. detect = the gate must fail it at the floor; ignore = it must pass clean.

fixtures.text:
FROM node:latest
USER root
RUN curl http://example.com/install.sh | sh
fixtures.reason: the classic trifecta: unpinned base, root user, remote pipe-to-shell

fixtures.text:
FROM node:18@sha256:abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890
USER root
HEALTHCHECK CMD true
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
fixtures.reason: pinned base but the final user is root

fixtures.text:
FROM node:latest
USER 10001
HEALTHCHECK CMD true
CMD ["node", "server.js"]

FROM node:18@sha256:abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890
ADD https://example.com/app.tar.gz /opt/app.tar.gz
USER 10001
HEALTHCHECK CMD true
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
fixtures.reason: ADD from a remote URL bypasses checksum verification

fixtures.text:
FROM node:18@sha256:abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890
ENV AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY
USER 10001
HEALTHCHECK CMD true
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
fixtures.reason: credential baked into an image layer

fixtures.text:
FROM node:18@sha256:abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890
USER 10001
RUN echo "no remote fetch, no curl|sh"
HEALTHCHECK CMD true
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
fixtures.reason: digest-pinned base, non-root user, no remote execution — must pass the gate
